engines.name: velocitee metal provisioning engine
engines.short: Bare-metal provisioning — PXE boot + unattended OS install (Proxmox VE, Ubuntu Server). Two backends: `builtin` seed stack or `maas` (optional).
engines.long: Turns bare, unconfigured hardware into a provisioned machine running Proxmox VE or Ubuntu Server. The default `builtin` backend runs a containerised seed stack (dnsmasq + nginx + iPXE + cloud-init) on a laptop or a Pi — no external services required. The optional `maas` backend hands provisioning off to an existing Canonical MAAS deployment for operators who already run one.

backends.summary: Containerised seed stack on the local machine. dnsmasq + nginx + iPXE + cloud-init. No external services.
Hands provisioning off to an existing Canonical MAAS region+rack via the REST API.

engines.name: velocitee network configuration engine
engines.short: Network configuration — OPNsense VM, VLANs, DHCP, DNS, firewall. Provisioner-agnostic via the renderer registry. Discovery + Path B (`vne join`) for existing networks.
engines.long: Two paths from one CLI. `vne deploy` provisions OPNsense as a VM on Proxmox VE, then configures VLANs, DHCP, DNS, and a baseline firewall, with an automated verification gate before declaring success. `vne join` discovers the network you're already on, identifies the gateway, picks a router adapter (OPNsense, unmanaged catch-all, or pluggable), and writes a join-mode manifest VSE/VLE can consume — read-only end-to-end.

backends.summary: Pure Python — talks Proxmox + OPNsense REST directly. Owns its own state file.
OpenTofu does VM creation, Ansible does network config. Hard phase gate between the two.

engines.name: velocitee services configuration engine
engines.short: Services — containerised stack deployment, idempotent configuration
engines.long: Deploys containerised services onto the verified network using idempotent Ansible roles, driven by a config bundle. Designed to be re-run safely at any time — services converge on the declared state rather than being imperatively installed once.

engines.name: velocitee lifecycle engine
engines.short: Lifecycle — monitoring, drift detection, auto-docs, single-command repair
engines.long: Monitoring, auto-documentation generated from the chain of handoff manifests, configuration drift detection against the declared state, and single-command repair.
